notes: v1.6.98 — TROUBLESHOOT ASSISTANT: an offline contextual front door over the embedded HW Matrix, launched from a BLOCKED phase on a rack. INTENT: a tech hitting a blocked phase had the diagnostic knowledge scattered across four surfaces (offline hwMatrix browser, online Voice Assistant, Ghost Echo field memory, Issue Log). This ties them into one OODA flow scoped to THIS rack — no manual filtering, 100% offline (no AI dependency, fits the Prime Directive), derives purely from data PHANTOM already has (matrix + rack slots + phase state + ghost echo). ENTRY: a 🔧 TROUBLESHOOT button now appears on any phase card whose status is 'blocked' (deploy_showRackDetail), as the primary action beside a demoted UNBLOCK. FLOW: ta_open(deployId,rackId,phaseId) derives the rack platform via ge_deriveHardwareType -> ta_platformFromHw (H100/H200/GB or null), reads the blocked phaseType, and opens a bottom-sheet (#ta-sheet) sibling to the hwMatrix sheet. ORIENT: a context header (rack · platform · BLOCKED: phase + blocker note), any Ghost Echoes for this hardware+phase pinned 'FROM THE FIELD' on top (ge_findQuirks), then a ranked fault shortlist (ta_rank: platform-filtered via ta_modelMatches B/H/X/G, kept if the entry's category is in TA_PHASE_CATS[phaseType], ordered by that map's priority then matrix order, capped 8). A search box widens to the FULL matrix (both platform+phase filters dropped, same predicate as hwMatrix). ACT: '✓ THIS FIXED IT — UNBLOCK' calls deploy_advancePhase(phase,'in_progress'), closes, re-renders rack detail, then offers the existing Ghost Echo capture modal (ge_openCaptureModal) so the fix becomes field memory for the next tech (skippable — phase stays unblocked either way). TA_PHASE_CATS map: mechanical->[liquid,led], power->[power,led], network->[transceiver,infiniband,nvlink], compute->[nvlink,led,liquid], validation->[all six]. REUSE: extracted hwMatrix_entryHtml(item) shared by both the hwMatrix reference sheet and this one (behavior-preserving — hwMatrix unchanged); reuses ge_deriveHardwareType / ge_findQuirks / ge_openCaptureModal, deploy_advancePhase, deploy_showRackDetail, and the global .hwm-* shell + .hwm-entry card CSS. NEW CSS: #ta-sheet overlay (mirrors #hw-matrix-sheet) + .ta-blocker-note/.ta-echo*/​.ta-foot* = 11 rule blocks (block-0 braces 2494 -> 2505, +11, balanced). The ranked logic was unit-tested via a temporary node harness (platform mapping, category ordering, platform filtering, empty-match) — ALL PASS — then embedded verbatim and the temp test deleted. EDGE: no derivable platform -> 'platform: unknown', phase-filtered all-models; empty platform+phase match -> 'search the full matrix' message (never a dead end); null _flat guarded; unblock double-tap debounced by deploy_advancePhase. SCOPE (YAGNI): no AI escalation, no new diagnostic content, no decision-tree branching, no global/standalone entry this ship (blocked-phase only), hwMatrix behavior unchanged. VERIFIED: node --check 2/2 inline scripts + sw.js, version.json valid, block-0 braces 2505 balanced (+11 reported), three stamps at v1.6.98. Built in Claude Code on 90e9fce (v1.6.97) per the one-builder rule; spec at docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-11-troubleshooting-assistant-design.md, plan at docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-11-troubleshooting-assistant.md (both untracked per repo convention). Operator browser walkthrough pending (blocked network phase on H100 -> shortlist -> search widen -> unblock+echo -> generic-rack + empty-match edges -> hwMatrix regression); web-Claude post-merge parity review pending.
